title: dryopteris namegatae
intro: Dryopteris namegatae, commonly known as Namegata fern, is a type of species in the Dryopteris genus, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that is native to Japan. Research on the health benefits of Dryopteris namegatae suggests it may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could potentially help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, which may be responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The rhizome of Dryopteris namegatae contains flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of this plant are rich in triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. side_effects: Dryopteris namegatae can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems.
